metadata.summary: Meta audience construction: demographics, interests, custom audiences (pixel/CRM/engagement), lookalikes, exclusions, reach estimation. Core rule: exclude recent purchasers from prospecting.

content.title: Meta Audience Targeting: Demographics, Interests, Custom Audiences, and Lookalikes
content.summary: Meta audience construction: demographics, interests, custom audiences (pixel/CRM/engagement), lookalikes, exclusions, reach estimation. Core rule: exclude recent purchasers from prospecting.

why.p: Meta's auction system allocates impressions based on audience overlap, bid, and relevance. Using the wrong audience type — or forgetting exclusions — inflates CPAs and triggers audience fatigue. Custom audiences built from pixel events or hashed CRM data consistently outperform broad interest targeting for retargeting. Lookalikes built from high-value source audiences (purchasers, not all visitors) produce 2-5x better ROAS than interest-only cold targeting.

list.item: Building or auditing any Meta ad set audience via the Marketing API
Creating custom audiences from pixel events, customer lists, or engagement sources
Generating lookalike audiences from a qualified source audience
Constructing full-funnel audience stacks (cold → warm → hot)
Estimating reach before launching using the `reachestimate` endpoint
Setting up Special Ad Category campaigns (housing, credit, employment) with restricted targeting
Campaign or ad set creation structure — use `ads-meta-campaign-setup` for the full three-tier setup
Budget allocation across audiences — use `ads-budget-optimization`
Instagram-specific placement targeting — use `instagram-ads` for placement-level API details
Audience analysis or reporting post-launch — this covers construction only

section.name: demographic-targeting
section.title: Meta Demographic Targeting
section.p: Geographic, age/gender, language, and device targeting parameters for Meta ad set targeting JSON.
section.title: Geographic Targeting

rule.statement: Always specify at least one geo_locations entry; Meta requires a location to serve any ad.
rule.rationale: Without a location constraint, ad sets enter review indefinitely and fail to deliver.

example.note: Countries use ISO 2-letter codes. City keys are Meta place IDs from the search API.

section.title: Age and Gender

rule.statement: Set age_min to 18 at minimum; Meta does not allow targeting under 18.
rule.rationale: Under-18 targeting triggers policy rejection and account flags regardless of product type.

example.note: Gender codes: 1 = Male, 2 = Female. Omit genders array to target all genders.

section.title: Language Targeting
Device Targeting

section.name: interests-behaviors
section.title: Interest and Behavior Targeting
section.p: Interest lookup API, behavior targeting, and flexible/layered logic operators for Meta ad sets.
section.title: Interest Search API

rule.statement: Look up interest IDs via the search API before hardcoding them — interest IDs do not change but names and audience sizes do.
rule.rationale: Using stale interest data from documentation produces silent mismatches; the search API returns current size bounds needed for audience sizing decisions.

example.note: Response includes audience_size_lower_bound and upper_bound — use these to verify size is in the 500K-5M target range.

section.title: Layered Targeting (AND Logic)
section.p: All conditions in the root targeting object use AND logic — users must match all criteria.

example.note: Result: US users aged 25-45 who are interested in Entrepreneurship AND are Engaged Shoppers.

section.title: Flexible Targeting (OR Logic)
section.p: Use flexible_spec to apply OR logic within the interest/behavior layer.

example.note: Result: users who match ANY of the listed interests.

section.title: Narrow Targeting (AND within OR)

example.note: Result: (Interest A) OR (Interest B AND Behavior C) — useful for narrowing broad interests with behavioral signals.

section.name: custom-audiences
section.title: Custom Audience Creation
section.p: API calls for website pixel audiences, CRM customer list uploads, engagement audiences, and app activity audiences.
section.title: Website Custom Audiences

rule.statement: Build website custom audiences (WCA) from pixel events, not just from all-page-visitors — event-based audiences produce 3-5x better retargeting conversion rates.
rule.rationale: All-visitor audiences include low-intent traffic (bots, accidental clicks). Event-based audiences (product viewers, cart abandoners) signal actual purchase intent.

section.title: CRM Customer List Upload

rule.statement: Hash all PII fields (email, phone, name) with SHA256 before uploading — never send plaintext PII to the API.
rule.rationale: Meta's API requires hashed data; plaintext PII in transit violates GDPR/CCPA and Meta's data policy, risking account suspension.

example.note: More schema fields = higher match rate (target 50-80%). Minimum 100 matched users to activate.

section.title: Engagement Audiences
section.p: Create audiences from Facebook Page engagers, Instagram Business Profile engagers, video viewers (by watch percentage), and lead form openers.

section.name: lookalike-audiences
section.title: Lookalike Audiences
section.p: Lookalike audience creation, sizing guidelines, multi-country specs, and source quality rules.

rule.statement: Use purchasers (not all website visitors) as the lookalike source — purchaser-based lookalikes consistently outperform visitor-based ones by 2-4x ROAS.
rule.rationale: Lookalike quality depends entirely on source signal quality. Visitor audiences include high bounce rates; purchaser audiences encode the intent pattern you actually want to replicate.

section.title: Sizing Guidelines

list.item: 1% — smallest reach, highest similarity, use for testing and high-value products
2-3% — medium reach, good quality, use for scaling proven campaigns
5-10% — largest reach, lower similarity, use for mass market awareness only

rule.statement: Test 1%, 3%, and 5% lookalikes simultaneously with equal budgets for 7 days before committing to a single size.
rule.rationale: Optimal lookalike size varies by product price point and audience depth; testing is cheaper than assuming the 1% is always best.

section.title: Multi-Country Lookalikes

example.note: Multi-country lookalike creates separate similarity models per country using the same source.

section.name: exclusions-reach
section.title: Exclusions, Reach Estimation, and Special Categories
section.p: Exclusion targeting JSON, reach estimation API, audience size guidelines, and Special Ad Category restrictions.
section.title: Exclusion Targeting

rule.statement: Exclude recent purchasers (last 180 days) from all prospecting ad sets — this is a mandatory exclusion, not optional.
rule.rationale: Serving prospecting ads to existing customers wastes budget, increases frequency for people who have already converted, and distorts CPA reporting.

section.title: Reach Estimation API

example.note: Response: users_lower_bound, users_upper_bound, estimate_dau. Target 500K-5M for interest targeting.

list.item: Under 1K — limited delivery, audience too narrow
1K-10K — acceptable for niche/B2B targeting
10K-500K — optimal for most campaigns
500K-5M — broad interest targeting range
Over 10M — consider narrowing with behaviors

section.title: Special Ad Categories

rule.statement: Declare special_ad_categories at campaign creation for housing, employment, or credit ads — adding it retroactively requires campaign recreation.
rule.rationale: Meta enforces Special Ad Category restrictions at the campaign level; campaigns created without the flag cannot be retroactively converted and must be rebuilt.

list.item: No age targeting (auto-set to 18-65+)
No gender targeting
Location radius minimum 15 miles
No exclusions based on demographics
Custom and lookalike audiences based on demographics are disallowed

section.name: limitations-and-gotchas
section.title: Limitations and Agent Gotchas
Core Limitations

list.item: Detailed-targeting deprecation: Meta has removed thousands of interest/behavior categories (2022-2024); agents using interest IDs from old playbooks find them invalid.
Custom Audience match rates: 50-80% typical; below this, pixel/CRM hashing or PII coverage is broken.
Lookalike sizing: 1% audiences in small countries (e.g., Estonia) are too small to deliver; minimum source 100 users; ideal 1000-50,000.
Special Ad Categories: housing/credit/employment campaigns lose age, gender, ZIP-radius, and lookalike creation — entire audience playbooks invalidated.
Audience overlap: Meta now auto-merges overlapping audiences within a campaign — manual A/B tests may not isolate variables.
Customer File source attribute (USER_PROVIDED_ONLY vs BOTH_USER_AND_PARTNER_PROVIDED) affects compliance; misclassification triggers takedowns.
Engagement audiences (video viewers, page engagers) only retain up to 365 days, with 180 days being the practical limit.

section.title: Agentic Workflow Considerations
section.p: A subagent owns the audience lifecycle: classify CRM segments, hash PII, upload via Customer File API, wait for size estimate, build lookalikes, set up exclusion logic across campaigns. Daily: refresh dynamic seed audiences (purchasers last 90d), check size estimates, monitor lookalike-source freshness. Human-in-loop: PII consent verification, exclusion-list approval (don't accidentally exclude prospects), Special Ad Category sign-off. Hashing must be done client-side BEFORE upload — never send raw PII to Meta.
section.title: Agent-Specific Gotchas

list.item: PII hashing in shell pipelines: openssl dgst -sha256 output varies by case sensitivity; agents must normalize to lowercase before hashing.
Audience size estimates are ranges, not exact; APIs return lower_bound and upper_bound; agents should use lower_bound for conservative planning.
Lookalike source freshness matters: a purchaser lookalike built from Q1 data stales by Q3; agents should refresh lookalike sources every 60-90 days.
Exclusions are not transitive: if you exclude audience A from audience B and audience B from audience C, audience A is not automatically excluded from C.
Custom audience payload validation happens asynchronously; API returns success immediately, but matching errors surface in the UI 12-24 hours later.
Broad audiences (10M+) trigger system-level delivery quality penalties; agents should narrow to 500K-5M whenever possible.

template.purpose: Naming convention for audiences: type, source, timeframe, size
